1	"""
2	Core data models for the Noosphere knowledge system.
3	
4	Every model is a Pydantic BaseModel for serialization, validation, and
5	JSON round-tripping to disk. The fundamental unit is the Claim — an atomic
6	proposition extracted from a transcript, attributed to a speaker, and
7	positioned in embedding space.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	from datetime import date, datetime
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import Optional, Any, Literal, NewType
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ConfigDict
15	import uuid
16	
17	
18	# ── Enums ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19	
20	class RelationType(str, Enum):
21	    """Semantic relationship between two claims or principles."""
22	    SUPPORTS = "supports"           # A provides evidence/reasoning for B
23	    CONTRADICTS = "contradicts"     # A is logically incompatible with B
24	    REFINES = "refines"             # A is a more precise version of B
25	    INSTANTIATES = "instantiates"   # A is a specific case of general B
26	    EXTENDS = "extends"             # A adds new scope to B
27	    ANALOGIZES = "analogizes"       # A draws structural parallel to B
28	    PRESUPPOSES = "presupposes"     # A requires B to be true
29	    QUALIFIES = "qualifies"         # A limits or conditions B
30	
31	
32	class Discipline(str, Enum):
33	    """Knowledge domains tracked by Theseus."""
34	    PHILOSOPHY = "Philosophy"
35	    PHYSICS = "Physics"
36	    AI = "AI"
37	    ENTREPRENEURSHIP = "Entrepreneurship"
38	    VC = "VC"
39	    ART = "Art & Film"
40	    LITERATURE = "Literature"
41	    MATHEMATICS = "Mathematics"
42	    ECONOMICS = "Economics"
43	    HISTORY = "History"
44	    EPISTEMOLOGY = "Epistemology"
45	    ETHICS = "Ethics"
46	    POLITICAL_PHILOSOPHY = "Political Philosophy"
47	    STRATEGY = "Strategy"
48	
49	
50	class ConvictionLevel(str, Enum):
51	    """How strongly a principle is held, inferred from discourse patterns."""
52	    AXIOM = "axiom"                 # Foundational, never questioned
53	    STRONG = "strong"               # Consistently asserted with emphasis
54	    MODERATE = "moderate"           # Asserted but open to refinement
55	    EXPLORATORY = "exploratory"     # Tentatively proposed, being tested
56	    CONTESTED = "contested"         # Actively debated among founders
57	
58	
59	# ── Round 3: Freshness & Decay (defined early for use in existing models) ────
60	
61	class Freshness(str, Enum):
62	    """Freshness status for revalidation tracking."""
63	    FRESH = "fresh"
64	    AGING = "aging"
65	    STALE = "stale"
66	    RETIRED = "retired"
67	
68	
69	# ── Pre-existing enums (needed by store / coherence) ────────────────────────
70	
71	class CoherenceVerdict(str, Enum):
72	    COHERE = "cohere"
73	    CONTRADICT = "contradict"
74	    UNRESOLVED = "unresolved"
75	
76	
77	class ConfidenceTier(str, Enum):
78	    FOUNDER = "founder"
79	    HIGH = "high"
80	    MODERATE = "moderate"
81	    LOW = "low"
82	    SPECULATIVE = "speculative"
83	
84	
85	class AdversarialChallengeStatus(str, Enum):
86	    PENDING = "pending"
87	    EVALUATED = "evaluated"
88	    ADDRESSED = "addressed"
89	    FATAL = "fatal"
90	    SURVIVED = "survived"
91	    FALLEN = "fallen"
92	
93	
94	class PredictiveClaimStatus(str, Enum):
95	    DRAFT = "draft"
96	    ACTIVE = "active"
97	    RESOLVED = "resolved"
98	    EXPIRED = "expired"
99	
100	
101	# ── Pre-existing data models (needed by store / coherence / adversarial) ────
102	
103	
104	class SixLayerScore(BaseModel):
105	    consistency: float = 0.0
106	    argumentation: float = 0.0
107	    probabilistic: float = 0.0
108	    geometric: float = 0.0
109	    information: float = 0.0
110	    judge: float = 0.0
111	
112	
113	class Artifact(BaseModel):
114	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
115	    uri: str = ""
116	    mime_type: str = ""
117	    byte_length: int = 0
118	    content_sha256: str = ""
119	    title: str = ""
120	    author: str = ""
121	    source_date: Optional[date] = None
122	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
123	    effective_at: Optional[datetime] = None
124	    superseded_at: Optional[datetime] = None
125	    effective_at_inferred: bool = True
126	    license_status: Optional[str] = "unknown"
127	    literature_connector: Optional[str] = ""
128	
129	
130	class Chunk(BaseModel):
131	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
132	    artifact_id: str = ""
133	    start_offset: int = 0
134	    end_offset: int = 0
135	    text: str = ""
136	    metadata: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict)
137	
138	
139	class CoherenceEvaluationPayload(BaseModel):
140	    final_verdict: CoherenceVerdict = CoherenceVerdict.UNRESOLVED
141	    aggregator_verdict: CoherenceVerdict = CoherenceVerdict.UNRESOLVED
142	    prior_scores: SixLayerScore = Field(default_factory=SixLayerScore)
143	    layer_verdicts: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict)
144	
145	
146	class DriftEvent(BaseModel):
147	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
148	    target_id: str = ""
149	    observed_at: date = Field(default_factory=date.today)
150	    drift_score: float = 0.0
151	
152	
153	class Entity(BaseModel):
154	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
155	    canonical_key: Optional[str] = None
156	    label: str = ""
157	    entity_type: str = ""
158	
159	
160	class ResearchSuggestion(BaseModel):
161	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
162	    title: str = ""
163	    summary: str = ""
164	
165	
166	class ReviewItem(BaseModel):
167	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
168	    claim_a_id: str = ""
169	    claim_b_id: str = ""
170	    reason: str = ""
171	    status: str = "open"
172	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
173	
174	
175	class AdversarialChallenge(BaseModel):
176	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
177	    conclusion_id: str = ""
178	    cluster_fingerprint: str = ""
179	    content_hash: str = ""
180	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
181	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
182	
183	
184	class VoiceProfile(BaseModel):
185	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
186	    canonical_name: str = ""
187	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
188	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
189	
190	
191	class VoicePhaseRecord(BaseModel):
192	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
193	    voice_id: str = ""
194	
195	
196	class CitationRecord(BaseModel):
197	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
198	    firm_claim_id: str = ""
199	    voice_id: str = ""
200	
201	
202	class RelativePositionMap(BaseModel):
203	    conclusion_id: str = ""
204	
205	
206	class ReadingQueueEntry(BaseModel):
207	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
208	    status: str = "queued"
209	    rationale: str = ""
210	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
211	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
212	
213	
214	class PredictiveClaim(BaseModel):
215	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
216	    author_key: Optional[str] = ""
217	    artifact_id: Optional[str] = ""
218	    status: PredictiveClaimStatus = PredictiveClaimStatus.DRAFT
219	    source_claim_id: str = ""
220	    voice_id: str = ""
221	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
222	
223	
224	class PredictionResolution(BaseModel):
225	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
226	    predictive_claim_id: str = ""
227	    resolved_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
228	
229	
230	def voice_canonical_key(name: str) -> str:
231	    return name.lower().strip().replace(" ", "_")
232	
233	
234	# ── Core Data Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
235	
236	class Speaker(BaseModel):
237	    """A participant in the podcast."""
238	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
239	    name: str
240	    role: str = "founder"  # founder | guest | moderator
241	
242	
243	class TranscriptSegment(BaseModel):
244	    """A contiguous segment of speech from one speaker."""
245	    speaker: Speaker
246	    text: str
247	    start_time: Optional[float] = None   # seconds from episode start
248	    end_time: Optional[float] = None
249	    episode_id: str = ""
250	
251	
252	class Claim(BaseModel):
253	    """
254	    An atomic proposition extracted from a transcript segment.
255	
256	    This is the fundamental unit of analysis. A claim is a single
257	    assertoric sentence that can be true or false, attributed to a
258	    speaker, and positioned in embedding space.
259	    """
260	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
261	    text: str                                   # The proposition itself
262	    speaker: Speaker                            # Who said it
263	    episode_id: str                             # Which episode
264	    episode_date: date                          # When
265	    segment_context: str = ""                   # Surrounding paragraph for disambiguation
266	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []          # Relevant domains
267	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None     # SBERT embedding vector
268	    confidence: float = 1.0                     # Extraction confidence (0-1)
269	    timestamp_seconds: Optional[float] = None   # Position in episode
270	    source_id: str = ""                         # Source artifact or chunk ID
271	    voice_id: str = ""                          # Voice profile ID
272	    # Round 3 additions
273	    freshness: Freshness = Freshness.FRESH
274	    last_validated_at: Optional[datetime] = None
275	
276	
277	class Principle(BaseModel):
278	    """
279	    A distilled, stable belief held by the firm.
280	
281	    Principles are derived from clusters of claims across multiple episodes.
282	    They represent the firm's enduring intellectual commitments — the things
283	    the founders believe deeply enough to act on repeatedly.
284	    """
285	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
286	    text: str                                   # Canonical statement
287	    description: str = ""                       # Elaborated explanation
288	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []
289	    conviction: ConvictionLevel = ConvictionLevel.MODERATE
290	    conviction_score: float = 0.5               # Continuous 0-1
291	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None
292	
293	    # Evidence trail
294	    supporting_claims: list[str] = []           # Claim IDs
295	    first_appeared: Optional[date] = None
296	    last_reinforced: Optional[date] = None
297	    mention_count: int = 0
298	
299	    # Coherence metrics (from the 6-layer engine)
300	    coherence_score: Optional[float] = None     # Composite Coh(Γ)
301	    consistency_score: Optional[float] = None   # S₁: formal consistency
302	    argumentation_score: Optional[float] = None # S₂: grounded extension ratio
303	    probabilistic_score: Optional[float] = None # S₃: Roche's measure
304	    geometric_score: Optional[float] = None     # S₄: embedding coherence

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import contextvars
4	import hashlib
5	import json
6	import logging
7	import os
8	import subprocess
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	from functools import wraps
11	from typing import Any, Callable
12	from uuid import uuid4
13	
14	from pydantic import BaseModel
15	
16	from noosphere.models import (
17	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
18	    Method,
19	    MethodImplRef,
20	    MethodInvocation,
21	    MethodType,
22	)
23	from noosphere.methods._hooks import _FAILURE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _PRE_HOOKS
24	from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	CORRELATION_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
29	    "CORRELATION_ID", default=""
30	)
31	TENANT_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
32	    "TENANT_ID", default="default"
33	)
34	
35	_store_factory: Callable[..., Any] | None = None
36	
37	
38	def set_store_factory(factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
39	    global _store_factory
40	    _store_factory = factory
41	
42	
43	def _get_store() -> Any:
44	    if _store_factory is not None:
45	        try:
46	            return _store_factory()
47	        except Exception:
48	            return None
49	    return None
50	
51	
52	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
53	    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
54	        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
55	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
56	
57	
58	def _current_git_sha() -> str:
59	    sha = os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA")
60	    if sha:
61	        return sha
62	    try:
63	        result = subprocess.run(
64	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
65	            capture_output=True,
66	            text=True,
67	            timeout=5,
68	        )
69	        if result.returncode == 0:
70	            return result.stdout.strip()
71	    except Exception:
72	        pass
73	    return "unknown"
74	
75	
76	def _current_correlation_id() -> str:
77	    return CORRELATION_ID.get()
78	
79	
80	def _current_tenant_id() -> str:
81	    return TENANT_ID.get()
82	
83	
84	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
85	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
86	        if isinstance(data, schema):
87	            return data
88	        return schema.model_validate(data)
89	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
90	        try:
91	            import jsonschema as _js
92	
93	            _js.validate(data, schema)
94	        except ImportError:
95	            pass
96	    return data
97	
98	
99	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
100	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
101	
102	
103	def _run_failure_path(
104	    spec: Method,
105	    inv: MethodInvocation,
106	    validated_input: Any,
107	    exc: BaseException,
108	) -> None:
109	    inv = inv.model_copy(
110	        update={
111	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
112	            "succeeded": False,
113	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
114	        }
115	    )
116	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
117	        try:
118	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
119	        except Exception:
120	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
121	    store = _get_store()
122	    if store is not None:
123	        try:
124	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
125	        except Exception:
126	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
127	
128	
129	def register_method(
130	    *,
131	    name: str,
132	    version: str,
133	    method_type: MethodType,
134	    input_schema: Any,
135	    output_schema: Any,
136	    description: str,
137	    rationale: str,
138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator
284

0	"""
1	Temporal evolution tracking for the Noosphere system — the Brain of the Firm.
2	
3	This module tracks how the firm's principles evolve across weekly podcast episodes.
4	It detects emergence, strengthening, drift, refinement, and abandonment of principles.
5	
6	Key classes:
7	- TemporalTracker: Records and retrieves principle states across time
8	- EvolutionAnalyzer: Generates reports on ideological trajectory and convergence
9	- ConvictionEstimator: Computes conviction scores from multiple signals
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import json
15	import math
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple
20	from collections import defaultdict
21	
22	import numpy as np
23	from numpy.typing import NDArray
24	from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
25	
26	from noosphere.models import (
27	    TemporalSnapshot,
28	    Principle,
29	    Episode,
30	    Claim,
31	    DriftEvent,
32	    RelationType,
33	)
34	from noosphere.ontology import OntologyGraph
35	
36	
37	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
38	
39	logger = get_logger(__name__)
40	
41	
42	# ── TemporalTracker ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	
44	
45	class TemporalTracker:
46	    """
47	    Records and retrieves the temporal evolution of principles.
48	
49	    Maintains a history of principle states (conviction, embedding, mentions)
50	    across episodes, enabling the detection of emergence, drift, abandonment,
51	    convergence, and splits.
52	    """
53	
54	    def __init__(self, graph: OntologyGraph):
55	        """
56	        Initialize the temporal tracker.
57	
58	        Args:
59	            graph: The OntologyGraph to track over time.
60	        """
61	        self.graph = graph
62	        # principle_id -> list[TemporalSnapshot]
63	        self.snapshots: Dict[str, List[TemporalSnapshot]] = defaultdict(list)
64	        self.episode_count = 0
65	        logger.info("Initialized TemporalTracker")
66	
67	    def record_snapshot(
68	        self,
69	        principle_id: str,
70	        episode_id: str,
71	        date_: date,
72	        embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None,
73	    ) -> TemporalSnapshot:
74	        """
75	        Record the state of a principle at a point in time.
76	
77	        Computes conviction_score from current mention_count and evidence.
78	        Computes drift_from_origin (cosine distance between current embedding
79	        and the embedding when the principle first appeared).
80	
81	        Args:
82	            principle_id: The principle ID.
83	            episode_id: The episode ID in which this state was recorded.
84	            date_: The date of the episode.
85	            embedding: Optional embedding vector for the principle at this time.
86	                      If None, uses the principle's current embedding.
87	
88	        Returns:
89	            The created TemporalSnapshot.
90	
91	        Raises:
92	            ValueError: If principle not found in graph.
93	        """
94	        principle = self.graph.get_principle(principle_id)
95	        if principle is None:
96	            raise ValueError(f"Principle {principle_id} not found in graph")
97	
98	        # Use provided embedding or fall back to principle's current embedding
99	        snapshot_embedding = embedding or principle.embedding
100	
101	        # Compute drift from origin (if we have both embeddings)
102	        drift_from_origin = None
103	        if snapshot_embedding is not None and principle.embedding is not None:
104	            # Find the original embedding from first snapshot, if available
105	            if self.snapshots[principle_id]:
106	                original_embedding = self.snapshots[principle_id][0].embedding
107	                if original_embedding is not None:
108	                    drift_from_origin = self._cosine_distance(
109	                        snapshot_embedding, original_embedding
110	                    )
111	            else:
112	                # First snapshot: drift is 0
113	                drift_from_origin = 0.0
114	
115	        snapshot = TemporalSnapshot(
116	            principle_id=principle_id,
117	            episode_id=episode_id,
118	            date=date_,
119	            conviction_score=principle.conviction_score,
120	            mention_count_cumulative=principle.mention_count,
121	            embedding=snapshot_embedding,
122	            drift_from_origin=drift_from_origin,
123	        )
124	
125	        self.snapshots[principle_id].append(snapshot)
126	        logger.debug(
127	            f"Recorded snapshot for principle {principle_id} "
128	            f"on {date_} (conviction={snapshot.conviction_score:.2f})"
129	        )
130	        return snapshot
131	
132	    def get_history(self, principle_id: str) -> List[TemporalSnapshot]:
133	        """
134	        Get all snapshots for a principle, sorted chronologically.
135	
136	        Args:
137	            principle_id: The principle ID.
138	
139	        Returns:
140	            List of TemporalSnapshot objects, sorted by date ascending.
141	        """
142	        snapshots = self.snapshots.get(principle_id, [])
143	        return sorted(snapshots, key=lambda s: s.date)
144	
145	    def compute_conviction_trajectory(
146	        self, principle_id: str
147	    ) -> List[Tuple[date, float]]:
148	        """
149	        Compute the conviction score over time.
150	
151	        A rising trajectory means the principle is being reinforced.
152	        A falling trajectory means it's being questioned or abandoned.
153	
154	        Args:
155	            principle_id: The principle ID.
156	
157	        Returns:
158	            List of (date, conviction_score) tuples, sorted chronologically.
159	        """
160	        history = self.get_history(principle_id)
161	        trajectory = [(snap.date, snap.conviction_score) for snap in history]
162	        return trajectory
163	
164	    def compute_embedding_drift(
165	        self, principle_id: str
166	    ) -> List[Tuple[date, float]]:
167	        """
168	        Compute the cosine distance from the principle's original embedding over time.
169	
170	        Drift > 0.1 means the principle's MEANING is shifting even if
171	        the label hasn't changed.
172	
173	        Args:
174	            principle_id: The principle ID.
175	
176	        Returns:
177	            List of (date, drift_distance) tuples, sorted chronologically.
178	            Drift is the cosine distance from the first snapshot's embedding.
179	        """
180	        history = self.get_history(principle_id)
181	        if not history:
182	            return []
183	
184	        original_embedding = history[0].embedding
185	        if original_embedding is None:
186	            logger.warning(
187	                f"No embedding for principle {principle_id} at origin"
188	            )
189	            return []
190	
191	        drift_trajectory = []
192	        for snap in history:
193	            if snap.embedding is not None:
194	                distance = self._cosine_distance(snap.embedding, original_embedding)
195	                drift_trajectory.append((snap.date, distance))
196	
197	        return drift_trajectory
198	
199	    def detect_emergence(self, episode_id: str) -> List[Principle]:

1	"""Calibration metrics: Brier, log-loss, ECE, reliability, resolution, coverage."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import math
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from noosphere.models import CalibrationMetrics, OutcomeKind
9	
10	from noosphere.evaluation.outcomes import ResolutionResult
11	
12	_EPS = 1e-15
13	
14	
15	def brier_score(results: list[ResolutionResult]) -> float:
16	    if not results:
17	        return 0.0
18	    return sum(r.score for r in results) / len(results)
19	
20	
21	def log_loss(results: list[ResolutionResult]) -> float:
22	    if not results:
23	        return 0.0
24	    total = 0.0
25	    for r in results:
26	        if r.kind == OutcomeKind.BINARY:
27	            p = float(r.predicted)
28	            y = 1.0 if r.actual else 0.0
29	            p = max(_EPS, min(1.0 - _EPS, p))
30	            total += -(y * math.log(p) + (1.0 - y) * math.log(1.0 - p))
31	        elif r.kind == OutcomeKind.INTERVAL:
32	            total += r.score
33	        elif r.kind == OutcomeKind.PREFERENCE:
34	            if isinstance(r.predicted, dict):
35	                probs = r.predicted.get("probabilities", {})
36	                if probs and r.actual in probs:
37	                    p = max(_EPS, min(1.0 - _EPS, float(probs[r.actual])))
38	                    total += -math.log(p)
39	                else:
40	                    total += r.score
41	            else:
42	                total += r.score
43	    return total / len(results)
44	
45	
46	def reliability_bins(
47	    results: list[ResolutionResult], n_bins: int = 10
48	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
49	    bins: list[list[ResolutionResult]] = [[] for _ in range(n_bins)]
50	    for r in results:
51	        if r.kind == OutcomeKind.BINARY:
52	            p = float(r.predicted)
53	            idx = min(int(p * n_bins), n_bins - 1)
54	            bins[idx].append(r)
55	
56	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
57	    for i, bucket in enumerate(bins):
58	        lower = i / n_bins
59	        upper = (i + 1) / n_bins
60	        if not bucket:
61	            out.append({
62	                "bin_lower": round(lower, 4),
63	                "bin_upper": round(upper, 4),
64	                "count": 0,
65	                "mean_predicted": 0.0,
66	                "mean_observed": 0.0,
67	            })
68	            continue
69	        mean_pred = sum(float(r.predicted) for r in bucket) / len(bucket)
70	        mean_obs = sum(1.0 if r.actual else 0.0 for r in bucket) / len(bucket)
71	        out.append({
72	            "bin_lower": round(lower, 4),
73	            "bin_upper": round(upper, 4),
74	            "count": len(bucket),
75	            "mean_predicted": round(mean_pred, 6),
76	            "mean_observed": round(mean_obs, 6),
77	        })
78	    return out
79	
80	
81	def ece(results: list[ResolutionResult], n_bins: int = 10) -> float:
82	    bins_data = reliability_bins(results, n_bins=n_bins)
83	    total_count = sum(b["count"] for b in bins_data)
84	    if total_count == 0:
85	        return 0.0
86	    weighted_sum = 0.0
87	    for b in bins_data:
88	        if b["count"] == 0:
89	            continue
90	        weighted_sum += b["count"] * abs(b["mean_predicted"] - b["mean_observed"])
91	    return weighted_sum / total_count
92	
93	
94	def resolution_score(results: list[ResolutionResult]) -> float:
95	    if not results:
96	        return 0.0
97	    binary = [r for r in results if r.kind == OutcomeKind.BINARY]
98	    if not binary:
99	        return 0.0
100	    base_rate = sum(1.0 if r.actual else 0.0 for r in binary) / len(binary)
101	    return sum((float(r.predicted) - base_rate) ** 2 for r in binary) / len(binary)
102	
103	
104	def coverage(total_outcomes: int, resolved_count: int) -> float:
105	    if total_outcomes == 0:
106	        return 0.0
107	    return resolved_count / total_outcomes
108	
109	
110	def compute_metrics_for_kind(
111	    results: list[ResolutionResult],
112	    kind: OutcomeKind,
113	    total_outcomes: int,
114	) -> CalibrationMetrics:
115	    filtered = [r for r in results if r.kind == kind and r.resolved]
116	    return CalibrationMetrics(
117	        brier=round(brier_score(filtered), 6),
118	        log_loss=round(log_loss(filtered), 6),
119	        ece=round(ece(filtered), 6),
120	        reliability_bins=reliability_bins(filtered),
121	        resolution=round(resolution_score(filtered), 6),
122	        coverage=round(coverage(total_outcomes, len(filtered)), 6),
123	    )
124	
125	
126	def compute_metrics(
127	    results: list[ResolutionResult],
128	    total_outcomes_by_kind: dict[OutcomeKind, int],
129	) -> dict[OutcomeKind, CalibrationMetrics]:
130	    out: dict[OutcomeKind, CalibrationMetrics] = {}
131	    for kind, total in total_outcomes_by_kind.items():
132	        out[kind] = compute_metrics_for_kind(results, kind, total)
133	    return out
134

1	"""Read-only store proxy pinned to a TemporalCut."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from datetime import datetime, timezone
6	from typing import Any, Iterator, Optional
7	
8	from noosphere.models import (
9	    Artifact,
10	    Chunk,
11	    Claim,
12	    Conclusion,
13	    CorpusSelector,
14	    TemporalCut,
15	)
16	
17	
18	class EmbargoViolation(Exception):
19	    """Raised when a read reaches past the temporal cut boundary."""
20	
21	
22	def _ensure_tz(dt: datetime) -> datetime:
23	    if dt.tzinfo is None:
24	        return dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
25	    return dt
26	
27	
28	def _matches_selector(artifact: Artifact, sel: CorpusSelector) -> bool:
29	    if sel.tenant_id_filter is not None:
30	        if getattr(artifact, "tenant_id", None) not in sel.tenant_id_filter:
31	            return False
32	    if sel.artifact_kind_filter is not None:
33	        if artifact.mime_type not in sel.artifact_kind_filter:
34	            return False
35	    return True
36	
37	
38	class CorpusSlicer:
39	    """Frozen, read-only view of a Store pinned to a past date.
40	
41	    Every read goes through the cut's ``as_of`` predicate.  Objects created
42	    after the cut raise ``EmbargoViolation``.  Write methods are not proxied
43	    — calling them raises ``AttributeError``.
44	    """
45	
46	    PROXIED_READ_METHODS = frozenset({
47	        "get_artifact",
48	        "get_chunk",
49	        "get_claim",
50	        "get_conclusion",
51	        "get_embedding_vector",
52	        "get_drift_event",
53	        "list_claim_ids",
54	        "list_conclusions",
55	        "list_chunks_for_artifact",
56	        "list_drift_events",
57	        "get_temporal_cut",
58	        "list_outcomes_for_cut",
59	    })
60	
61	    def __init__(self, store: Any, cut: TemporalCut) -> None:
62	        self._store = store
63	        self._cut = cut
64	        self._as_of = _ensure_tz(cut.as_of)
65	
66	    @property
67	    def cut(self) -> TemporalCut:
68	        return self._cut
69	
70	    def _check_artifact_embargo(self, obj: Artifact, label: str) -> None:
71	        created = _ensure_tz(obj.created_at)
72	        if created > self._as_of:
73	            raise EmbargoViolation(
74	                f"{label} is after {self._as_of.isoformat()}"
75	            )
76	        if not _matches_selector(obj, self._cut.corpus_slice):
77	            raise EmbargoViolation(
78	                f"{label} does not match corpus_slice selector"
79	            )
80	
81	    def _check_created_at(self, obj: Any, label: str) -> None:
82	        created = getattr(obj, "created_at", None)
83	        if created is not None:
84	            if _ensure_tz(created) > self._as_of:
85	                raise EmbargoViolation(
86	                    f"{label} is after {self._as_of.isoformat()}"
87	                )
88	
89	    # ── Proxied reads ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
90	
91	    def get_artifact(self, artifact_id: str) -> Optional[Artifact]:
92	        obj = self._store.get_artifact(artifact_id)
93	        if obj is None:
94	            return None
95	        self._check_artifact_embargo(obj, artifact_id)
96	        return obj
97	
98	    def get_chunk(self, chunk_id: str) -> Optional[Chunk]:
99	        obj = self._store.get_chunk(chunk_id)
100	        if obj is None:
101	            return None
102	        art = self._store.get_artifact(obj.artifact_id)
103	        if art is not None:
104	            self._check_artifact_embargo(art, f"chunk:{chunk_id}/artifact:{obj.artifact_id}")
105	        return obj
106	
107	    def get_claim(self, claim_id: str) -> Optional[Claim]:
108	        obj = self._store.get_claim(claim_id)
109	        if obj is None:
110	            return None
111	        ep_date = getattr(obj, "episode_date", None)
112	        if ep_date is not None:
113	            from datetime import date as _date
114	            if isinstance(ep_date, _date):
115	                ep_dt = datetime(ep_date.year, ep_date.month, ep_date.day,
116	                                 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
117	                if ep_dt > self._as_of:
118	                    raise EmbargoViolation(
119	                        f"{claim_id} is after {self._as_of.isoformat()}"
120	                    )
121	        return obj
122	
123	    def get_conclusion(self, conclusion_id: str) -> Optional[Conclusion]:
124	        obj = self._store.get_conclusion(conclusion_id)
125	        if obj is None:
126	            return None
127	        self._check_created_at(obj, conclusion_id)
128	        return obj
129	
130	    def get_embedding_vector(self, embedding_id: str) -> Optional[list[float]]:
131	        return self._store.get_embedding_vector(embedding_id)
132	
133	    def get_drift_event(self, drift_id: str):
134	        obj = self._store.get_drift_event(drift_id)
135	        if obj is None:
136	            return None
137	        return obj
138	
139	    def list_claim_ids(self) -> list[str]:
140	        all_ids = self._store.list_claim_ids()
141	        filtered: list[str] = []
142	        for cid in all_ids:
143	            try:
144	                c = self.get_claim(cid)
145	                if c is not None:
146	                    filtered.append(cid)
147	            except EmbargoViolation:
148	                continue
149	        return filtered
150	
151	    def list_conclusions(self) -> list[Conclusion]:
152	        all_conc = self._store.list_conclusions()
153	        filtered: list[Conclusion] = []
154	        for c in all_conc:
155	            created = getattr(c, "created_at", None)
156	            if created is not None and _ensure_tz(created) > self._as_of:
157	                continue
158	            filtered.append(c)
159	        return filtered
160	
161	    def list_chunks_for_artifact(self, artifact_id: str) -> list[Chunk]:
162	        self.get_artifact(artifact_id)
163	        return self._store.list_chunks_for_artifact(artifact_id)
164	
165	    def list_drift_events(self, *, limit: int = 500):
166	        return self._store.list_drift_events(limit=limit)
167	
168	    def get_temporal_cut(self, cut_id: str):
169	        return self._store.get_temporal_cut(cut_id)
170	
171	    def list_outcomes_for_cut(self, cut_id: str):
172	        return self._store.list_outcomes_for_cut(cut_id)
173	
174	    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
175	        if name.startswith("_"):
176	            raise AttributeError(name)
177	        raise AttributeError(
178	            f"CorpusSlicer does not proxy '{name}'. "
179	            f"Only read operations are available under embargo."
180	        )

101	
102	        # Compute drift from origin (if we have both embeddings)
103	        drift_from_origin = None
104	        if snapshot_embedding is not None and principle.embedding is not None:
105	            # Find the original embedding from first snapshot, if available
106	            if self.snapshots[principle_id]:
107	                original_embedding = self.snapshots[principle_id][0].embedding
108	                if original_embedding is not None:
109	                    drift_from_origin = self._cosine_distance(
110	                        snapshot_embedding, original_embedding
111	                    )
112	            else:
113	                # First snapshot: drift is 0
114	                drift_from_origin = 0.0
115	
116	        snapshot = TemporalSnapshot(
117	            principle_id=principle_id,
118	            episode_id=episode_id,
119	            date=date_,
120	            conviction_score=principle.conviction_score,
121	            mention_count_cumulative=principle.mention_count,
122	            embedding=snapshot_embedding,
123	            drift_from_origin=drift_from_origin,
124	        )
125	
126	        self.snapshots[principle_id].append(snapshot)
127	        logger.debug(
128	            f"Recorded snapshot for principle {principle_id} "
129	            f"on {date_} (conviction={snapshot.conviction_score:.2f})"
130	        )
131	        return snapshot
132	
133	    def get_history(self, principle_id: str) -> List[TemporalSnapshot]:
134	        """
135	        Get all snapshots for a principle, sorted chronologically.
136	
137	        Args:
138	            principle_id: The principle ID.
139	
140	        Returns:
141	            List of TemporalSnapshot objects, sorted by date ascending.
142	        """
143	        snapshots = self.snapshots.get(principle_id, [])
144	        return sorted(snapshots, key=lambda s: s.date)
145	
146	    def compute_conviction_trajectory(
147	        self, principle_id: str
148	    ) -> List[Tuple[date, float]]:
149	        """
150	        Compute the conviction score over time.
151	
152	        A rising trajectory means the principle is being reinforced.
153	        A falling trajectory means it's being questioned or abandoned.
154	
155	        Args:
156	            principle_id: The principle ID.
157	
158	        Returns:
159	            List of (date, conviction_score) tuples, sorted chronologically.
160	        """
161	        history = self.get_history(principle_id)
162	        trajectory = [(snap.date, snap.conviction_score) for snap in history]
163	        return trajectory
164	
165	    def compute_embedding_drift(
166	        self, principle_id: str
167	    ) -> List[Tuple[date, float]]:
168	        """
169	        Compute the cosine distance from the principle's original embedding over time.
170	
171	        Drift > 0.1 means the principle's MEANING is shifting even if
172	        the label hasn't changed.
173	
174	        Args:
175	            principle_id: The principle ID.
176	
177	        Returns:
178	            List of (date, drift_distance) tuples, sorted chronologically.
179	            Drift is the cosine distance from the first snapshot's embedding.
180	        """
181	        history = self.get_history(principle_id)
182	        if not history:
183	            return []
184	
185	        original_embedding = history[0].embedding
186	        if original_embedding is None:
187	            logger.warning(
188	                f"No embedding for principle {principle_id} at origin"
189	            )
190	            return []
191	
192	        drift_trajectory = []
193	        for snap in history:
194	            if snap.embedding is not None:
195	                distance = self._cosine_distance(snap.embedding, original_embedding)
196	                drift_trajectory.append((snap.date, distance))
197	
198	        return drift_trajectory
199	
200	    def detect_emergence(self, episode_id: str) -> List[Principle]:
201	        """
202	        Identify principles that first appeared in this episode.
203	
204	        Args:
205	            episode_id: The episode ID.
206	
207	        Returns:
208	            List of Principle objects that have their first_appeared date
209	            matching this episode.
210	        """
211	        emerged = []
212	        for principle in self.graph.principles.values():
213	            # Get snapshots for this principle in this episode
214	            episode_snapshots = [
215	                s for s in self.snapshots.get(principle.id, [])
216	                if s.episode_id == episode_id
217	            ]
218	            # If this is the first mention (mention count jumped from 0),
219	            # it emerged in this episode
220	            if episode_snapshots and principle.mention_count >= 1:
221	                # Check if this is truly first appearance
222	                history = self.get_history(principle.id)
223	                if len(history) == 1:  # Only one snapshot = first appearance
224	                    emerged.append(principle)
225	
226	        return emerged
227	
228	    def detect_abandonment(
229	        self, lookback_episodes: int = 10, threshold: int = 0
230	    ) -> List[Principle]:
231	        """
232	        Identify principles that haven't been mentioned in the last N episodes.
233	
234	        Args:
235	            lookback_episodes: Number of recent episodes to consider.
236	            threshold: Conviction threshold (only flag principles below this).
237	
238	        Returns:
239	            List of Principle objects that appear abandoned.
240	        """
241	        abandoned = []
242	
243	        for principle in self.graph.principles.values():
244	            history = self.get_history(principle.id)
245	            if not history:
246	                continue
247	
248	            # Get the last N snapshots
249	            recent = history[-lookback_episodes:]
250	
251	            # Check if conviction is declining
252	            if len(recent) > 1:
253	                conviction_trend = [s.conviction_score for s in recent]
254	                # If the last conviction is at or below threshold, flag it
255	                if conviction_trend[-1] <= threshold:
256	                    abandoned.append(principle)
257	
258	        return abandoned
259	
260	    def detect_convergence(
261	        self, principle_id_a: str, principle_id_b: str
262	    ) -> Optional[float]:
263	        """
264	        Check if two principles are becoming more similar over time.
265	
266	        Returns the rate of convergence (negative = diverging).
267	
268	        Algorithm:
269	        1. Get embedding history for both principles
270	        2. Compute similarity between them at each timestep
271	        3. Fit a line to the similarity trend
272	        4. Return the slope (positive = converging, negative = diverging)
273	
274	        Args:
275	            principle_id_a: First principle ID.
276	            principle_id_b: Second principle ID.
277	
278	        Returns:
279	            The rate of convergence (slope). Positive means converging,
280	            negative means diverging. None if insufficient data.
281	        """
282	        history_a = self.get_history(principle_id_a)
283	        history_b = self.get_history(principle_id_b)
284	
285	        if len(history_a) < 2 or len(history_b) < 2:
286	            return None
287	
288	        # Find overlapping time period
289	        start_date = max(history_a[0].date, history_b[0].date)
290	        end_date = min(history_a[-1].date, history_b[-1].date)
291	
292	        if start_date >= end_date:
293	            return None
294	
295	        # Compute similarity at each overlapping snapshot
296	        similarities = []
297	        for snap_a, snap_b in zip(history_a, history_b):
298	            if snap_a.date < start_date or snap_a.date > end_date:
299	                continue
300	            if snap_b.date < start_date or snap_b.date > end_date:
301	                continue
302	
303	            if snap_a.embedding is not None and snap_b.embedding is not None:
304	                sim = 1.0 - self._cosine_distance(snap_a.embedding, snap_b.embedding)
305	                similarities.append((snap_a.date, sim))
306	
307	        if len(similarities) < 2:
308	            return None
309	
310	        # Fit a line: compute trend
311	        dates_numeric = np.array(
312	            [(s[0] - similarities[0][0]).days for s, _ in enumerate(similarities)]
313	        )
314	        sims = np.array([s[1] for s in similarities])
315	
316	        if len(dates_numeric) == 1:
317	            return None
318	
319	        # Linear regression: y = mx + b
320	        # We want the slope m
321	        coefficients = np.polyfit(dates_numeric, sims, 1)
322	        slope = coefficients[0]
323	
324	        logger.debug(
325	            f"Convergence rate between {principle_id_a} and {principle_id_b}: {slope:.4f}"
326	        )
327	        return float(slope)
328	
329	    def detect_splits(
330	        self, principle_id: str, threshold: float = 0.15
331	    ) -> List[Principle]:
332	        """
333	        Detect if a principle has "split" into two or more sub-principles.
334	
335	        Algorithm:
336	        1. Get all supporting claims for the principle
337	        2. Cluster them into sub-groups
338	        3. If clusters are distinct and stable over recent episodes,
339	           treat them as splits
340	        4. Return list of detected sub-principles
341	
342	        Args:
343	            principle_id: The principle ID to analyze.
344	            threshold: Distance threshold for detecting distinct clusters.
345	
346	        Returns:
347	            List of detected sub-principles (principles with embedding distance
348	            > threshold from the parent).
349	        """
350	        principle = self.graph.get_principle(principle_id)
351	        if principle is None or principle.embedding is None:
352	            return []
353	
354	        # Get all principles that reference this one
355	        potential_splits = []
356	        for other_principle in self.graph.principles.values():
357	            if other_principle.id == principle_id:
358	                continue
359	            if other_principle.embedding is None:
360	                continue
361	
362	            # Check if this principle's embedding has diverged significantly
363	            distance = self._cosine_distance(
364	                principle.embedding, other_principle.embedding
365	            )
366	
367	            # If distance > threshold, it's a potential split
368	            if distance > threshold:
369	                # Check if this principle mentions the parent
370	                related = self.graph.get_related(principle_id, depth=2)
371	                if other_principle.id in [r.id for r in related]:
372	                    potential_splits.append(other_principle)
373	
374	        return potential_splits
375	
376	    # ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
377	
378	    @staticmethod
379	    def _cosine_distance(
380	        embedding_a: list[float], embedding_b: list[float]
381	    ) -> float:
382	        """
383	        Compute cosine distance between two embeddings.
384	
385	        Distance = 1 - cosine_similarity
386	
387	        Args:
388	            embedding_a: First embedding vector.
389	            embedding_b: Second embedding vector.
390	
391	        Returns:
392	            Cosine distance (0 = identical, 1 = orthogonal, 2 = opposite).
393	        """
394	        a = np.array(embedding_a).reshape(1, -1)
395	        b = np.array(embedding_b).reshape(1, -1)
396	        similarity = cosine_similarity(a, b)[0, 0]
397	        return float(1.0 - similarity)
398

2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Any, Optional

8	from noosphere.models import Outcome, OutcomeKind
9	
10	
11	class ResolutionError(Exception):
12	    """Raised when resolution logic cannot proceed."""
13	
14	
15	@dataclass(frozen=True)
16	class ResolutionResult:
17	    """Result of resolving an outcome against a prediction."""
18	    outcome_id: str
19	    kind: OutcomeKind
20	    predicted: Any
21	    actual: Any
22	    score: float
23	    resolved: bool
24	    resolution_source: str
25	    detail: str = ""
26	
27	
28	def _resolve_binary(outcome: Outcome, prediction: Any) -> ResolutionResult:
29	    actual = bool(outcome.value)
30	    if isinstance(prediction, (int, float)):
31	        prob = float(prediction)
32	    elif isinstance(prediction, bool):
33	        prob = 1.0 if prediction else 0.0
34	    elif isinstance(prediction, dict) and "probability" in prediction:
35	        prob = float(prediction["probability"])
36	    else:
37	        raise ResolutionError(

39	        )
40	    score = (prob - (1.0 if actual else 0.0)) ** 2
41	    return ResolutionResult(
42	        outcome_id=outcome.outcome_id,
43	        kind=OutcomeKind.BINARY,
44	        predicted=prob,
45	        actual=actual,
46	        score=score,
47	        resolved=True,

49	    )

52	def _resolve_interval(outcome: Outcome, prediction: Any) -> ResolutionResult:
53	    if isinstance(outcome.value, (int, float)):
54	        actual_value = float(outcome.value)
55	    else:
56	        raise ResolutionError(
57	            f"Interval outcome value must be numeric, got {type(outcome.value)}"
58	        )
59	
60	    if isinstance(prediction, (int, float)):
61	        predicted_value = float(prediction)
62	        lower: Optional[float] = None
63	        upper: Optional[float] = None
64	    elif isinstance(prediction, dict):
65	        predicted_value = float(prediction.get("point", prediction.get("value", 0)))
66	        lower = prediction.get("lower")
67	        upper = prediction.get("upper")
68	        if lower is not None:
69	            lower = float(lower)
70	        if upper is not None:
71	            upper = float(upper)
72	    else:
73	        raise ResolutionError(
74	            f"Cannot interpret prediction for interval outcome: {prediction!r}"
75	        )
76	
77	    score = (predicted_value - actual_value) ** 2
78	    hit = True
79	    if lower is not None and upper is not None:
80	        hit = lower <= actual_value <= upper
81	
82	    return ResolutionResult(
83	        outcome_id=outcome.outcome_id,
84	        kind=OutcomeKind.INTERVAL,
85	        predicted=prediction,
86	        actual=actual_value,
87	        score=score,
88	        resolved=True,
89	        resolution_source=outcome.resolution_source,
90	        detail=f"interval_hit={hit}",
91	    )
92	
93	
94	def _resolve_preference(outcome: Outcome, prediction: Any) -> ResolutionResult:
95	    if not isinstance(outcome.value, str):
96	        raise ResolutionError(
97	            f"Preference outcome value must be a string (chosen option), got {type(outcome.value)}"
98	        )
99	    actual_choice = outcome.value
100	
101	    if isinstance(prediction, str):
102	        predicted_choice = prediction
103	        score = 0.0 if predicted_choice == actual_choice else 1.0
104	    elif isinstance(prediction, dict):
105	        predicted_choice = prediction.get("choice", "")
106	        probs = prediction.get("probabilities", {})
107	        if probs and actual_choice in probs:
108	            p = float(probs[actual_choice])
109	            score = (1.0 - p) ** 2
110	        else:
111	            score = 0.0 if predicted_choice == actual_choice else 1.0
112	    else:
113	        raise ResolutionError(
114	            f"Cannot interpret prediction for preference outcome: {prediction!r}"
115	        )
116	
117	    return ResolutionResult(
118	        outcome_id=outcome.outcome_id,
119	        kind=OutcomeKind.PREFERENCE,
120	        predicted=prediction,
121	        actual=actual_choice,
122	        score=score,
123	        resolved=True,
124	        resolution_source=outcome.resolution_source,
125	    )
126	
127	
128	_RESOLVERS = {
129	    OutcomeKind.BINARY: _resolve_binary,
130	    OutcomeKind.INTERVAL: _resolve_interval,
131	    OutcomeKind.PREFERENCE: _resolve_preference,
132	}
133	
134	
135	def resolve(outcome: Outcome, prediction: Any) -> ResolutionResult:
136	    """Resolve an outcome against a prediction.
137	
138	    Every outcome MUST carry ``resolution_source``; this function does
139	    NOT self-grade — it merely computes the score from the externally-
140	    provided resolution.
141	    """
142	    if not outcome.resolution_source:
143	        raise ResolutionError(
144	            f"Outcome {outcome.outcome_id} has no resolution_source; cannot self-grade"
145	        )
146	    resolver = _RESOLVERS.get(outcome.kind)
147	    if resolver is None:
148	        raise ResolutionError(f"No resolver for outcome kind {outcome.kind}")
149	    return resolver(outcome, prediction)

26	class MethodRegistry:
27	    def __init__(self) -> None:
28	        self._specs: dict[tuple[str, str], Method] = {}
29	        self._fns: dict[tuple[str, str], Callable] = {}
30	        self._emits_edges: dict[str, list[CascadeEdgeRelation]] = {}
31	
32	    def register(self, spec: Method, fn: Callable) -> None:
33	        key = (spec.name, spec.version)
34	        if key in self._specs:
35	            raise MethodCollisionError(
36	                f"Method {spec.name} v{spec.version} already registered"
37	            )
38	        self._specs[key] = spec
39	        self._fns[key] = fn
40	
41	    def set_emits_edges(
42	        self, method_id: str, edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]
43	    ) -> None:
44	        self._emits_edges[method_id] = list(edges)
45	
46	    def get_emits_edges(self, method_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdgeRelation]:
47	        return self._emits_edges.get(method_id, [])
48	
49	    def get(
50	        self,
51	        name: str,
52	        version: str = "latest",
53	        include_retired: bool = False,
54	    ) -> tuple[Method, Callable]:
55	        if version != "latest":
56	            key = (name, version)
57	            if key not in self._specs:
58	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
59	                    f"Method {name} v{version} not found"
60	                )
61	            spec = self._specs[key]
62	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
63	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
64	                    f"Method {name} v{version} is retired"
65	                )
66	            return spec, self._fns[key]
67	
68	        candidates: list[tuple[str, Method]] = []
69	        for (n, v), spec in self._specs.items():
70	            if n != name:
71	                continue
72	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
73	                continue
74	            candidates.append((v, spec))
75	
76	        if not candidates:
77	            raise MethodNotFoundError(f"No active method found for {name}")
78	
79	        candidates.sort(key=lambda x: _parse_semver(x[0]), reverse=True)
80	        best_version = candidates[0][0]
81	        return self._specs[(name, best_version)], self._fns[(name, best_version)]
82	
83	    def list(self, status_filter: str | None = None) -> list[Method]:
84	        specs = list(self._specs.values())
85	        if status_filter is not None:
86	            specs = [s for s in specs if s.status == status_filter]
87	        return specs
88

class OutcomeKind(str, Enum):
    """Type of outcome for evaluation."""
    BINARY = "binary"
    INTERVAL = "interval"
    PREFERENCE = "preference"


class Outcome(BaseModel):
    """A resolved outcome for calibration evaluation."""
    outcome_id: str
    kind: OutcomeKind
    event_ref: str
    resolution_source: str
    resolved_at: datetime
    value: Any

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class CorpusSelector(BaseModel):
    """Selector for corpus slicing."""
    as_of: datetime
    tenant_id_filter: Optional[list[str]] = None

class LicenseTag(str, Enum):
    """License tags for external corpora."""
    GJP_PUBLIC = "gjp_public"
    METACULUS_PUBLIC = "metaculus_public"
    CLAIM_REVIEW = "claim_review"
    REPLICATION_PUBLIC = "replication_public"
    CUSTOM = "custom"


class CorpusBundle(BaseModel):

    source: str
    content_hash: str
    local_path: str
    license: LicenseTag
    fetched_at: datetime

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)

    source: str

    resolved_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    outcome_type: OutcomeKind
    metadata: dict

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)

    method_ref: MethodRef

    failures: dict

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)

method_invocation_id: str
    confidence: float
    unresolved: bool
    established_at: datetime
    retracted_at: Optional[datetime] = None

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid')


# ── Round 3: Evaluation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class OutcomeKind(str, Enum):
    """Type of outcome for evaluation."""
    BINARY = "binary"
    INTERVAL = "interval"
    PREFERENCE = "preference"


class Outcome(BaseModel):
    """A resolved outcome for calibration evaluation."""
    outcome_id: str
    kind: OutcomeKind
    event_ref: str
    resolution_source: str
    resolved_at: datetime
    value: Any

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class CorpusSelector(BaseModel):
    """Selector for corpus slicing."""
    as_of: datetime
    tenant_id_filter: Optional[list[str]] = None
    artifact_kind_filter: Optional[list[str]] = None

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class TemporalCut(BaseModel):
    """A temporal slice of the corpus for evaluation."""
    cut_id: str
    as_of: datetime
    corpus_slice: CorpusSelector
    embargoed: CorpusSelector
    embedding_version_pin: str
    outcomes: list[Outcome]

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class CalibrationMetrics(BaseModel):
    """Calibration metrics for evaluation."""
    brier: float
    log_loss: float
    ece: float
    reliability_bins: list[dict]
    resolution: float
    coverage: float

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class CounterfactualEvalRun(BaseModel):
    """A counterfactual evaluation run."""
    run_id: str
    method_ref: MethodRef
    cut_id: str
    metrics: CalibrationMetrics
    prediction_refs: list[str]
    created_at: datetime

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


# ── Round 3: External Battery ────────────────────────────────────────────────

class LicenseTag(str, Enum):
    """License tags for external corpora."""
    GJP_PUBLIC = "gjp_public"
    METACULUS_PUBLIC = "metaculus_public"
    CLAIM_REVIEW = "claim_review"
    REPLICATION_PUBLIC = "replication_public"
    CUSTOM = "custom"


class CorpusBundle(BaseModel):
    """An external corpus bundle."""
    source: str
    content_hash: str
    local_path: str
    license: LicenseTag
    fetched_at: datetime

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class ExternalItem(BaseModel):
    """An item from an external corpus."""
    source: str
    source_id: str
    question_text: str
    as_of: datetime
    resolved_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    outcome_type: OutcomeKind
    metadata: dict

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class BatteryRunResult(BaseModel):
    """Result of running a battery test."""
    run_id: str
    corpus_name: str
    method_ref: MethodRef
    per_item_results: list[dict]
    metrics: CalibrationMetrics
    failures: dict

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


# ── Round 3: Inverse ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class ResolvedEvent(BaseModel):
    """A resolved event for inverse queries."""
    event_id: str
    description: str
    resolved_at: datetime
    evidence_refs: list[str]

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class InverseQuery(BaseModel):
    """An inverse query to find implications."""
    event: ResolvedEvent
    as_of: datetime
    methods: list[MethodRef]
    k: int = 50

1	"""Calibration metrics: Brier, log-loss, ECE, reliability, resolution, coverage."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import math
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from noosphere.models import CalibrationMetrics, OutcomeKind
9	
10	from noosphere.evaluation.outcomes import ResolutionResult
11	
12	_EPS = 1e-15
13	
14	
15	def brier_score(results: list[ResolutionResult]) -> float:
16	    if not results:
17	        return 0.0
18	    return sum(r.score for r in results) / len(results)
19	
20	
21	def log_loss(results: list[ResolutionResult]) -> float:
22	    if not results:
23	        return 0.0
24	    total = 0.0
25	    for r in results:
26	        if r.kind == OutcomeKind.BINARY:
27	            p = float(r.predicted)
28	            y = 1.0 if r.actual else 0.0
29	            p = max(_EPS, min(1.0 - _EPS, p))
30	            total += -(y * math.log(p) + (1.0 - y) * math.log(1.0 - p))
31	        elif r.kind == OutcomeKind.INTERVAL:
32	            total += r.score
33	        elif r.kind == OutcomeKind.PREFERENCE:
34	            if isinstance(r.predicted, dict):
35	                probs = r.predicted.get("probabilities", {})
36	                if probs and r.actual in probs:
37	                    p = max(_EPS, min(1.0 - _EPS, float(probs[r.actual])))
38	                    total += -math.log(p)
39	                else:
40	                    total += r.score
41	            else:
42	                total += r.score
43	    return total / len(results)
44	
45	
46	def reliability_bins(
47	    results: list[ResolutionResult], n_bins: int = 10
48	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
49	    bins: list[list[ResolutionResult]] = [[] for _ in range(n_bins)]
50	    for r in results:
51	        if r.kind == OutcomeKind.BINARY:
52	            p = float(r.predicted)
53	            idx = min(int(p * n_bins), n_bins - 1)
54	            bins[idx].append(r)
55	
56	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
57	    for i, bucket in enumerate(bins):
58	        lower = i / n_bins
59	        upper = (i + 1) / n_bins
60	        if not bucket:
61	            out.append({
62	                "bin_lower": round(lower, 4),
63	                "bin_upper": round(upper, 4),
64	                "count": 0,
65	                "mean_predicted": 0.0,
66	                "mean_observed": 0.0,
67	            })
68	            continue
69	        mean_pred = sum(float(r.predicted) for r in bucket) / len(bucket)
70	        mean_obs = sum(1.0 if r.actual else 0.0 for r in bucket) / len(bucket)
71	        out.append({
72	            "bin_lower": round(lower, 4),
73	            "bin_upper": round(upper, 4),
74	            "count": len(bucket),
75	            "mean_predicted": round(mean_pred, 6),
76	            "mean_observed": round(mean_obs, 6),
77	        })
78	    return out
79	
80	
81	def ece(results: list[ResolutionResult], n_bins: int = 10) -> float:
82	    bins_data = reliability_bins(results, n_bins=n_bins)
83	    total_count = sum(b["count"] for b in bins_data)
84	    if total_count == 0:
85	        return 0.0
86	    weighted_sum = 0.0
87	    for b in bins_data:
88	        if b["count"] == 0:
89	            continue
90	        weighted_sum += b["count"] * abs(b["mean_predicted"] - b["mean_observed"])
91	    return weighted_sum / total_count
92	
93	
94	def resolution_score(results: list[ResolutionResult]) -> float:
95	    if not results:
96	        return 0.0
97	    binary = [r for r in results if r.kind == OutcomeKind.BINARY]
98	    if not binary:
99	        return 0.0
100	    base_rate = sum(1.0 if r.actual else 0.0 for r in binary) / len(binary)
101	    return sum((float(r.predicted) - base_rate) ** 2 for r in binary) / len(binary)
102	
103	
104	def coverage(total_outcomes: int, resolved_count: int) -> float:
105	    if total_outcomes == 0:
106	        return 0.0
107	    return resolved_count / total_outcomes
108	
109	
110	def compute_metrics_for_kind(
111	    results: list[ResolutionResult],
112	    kind: OutcomeKind,
113	    total_outcomes: int,
114	) -> CalibrationMetrics:
115	    filtered = [r for r in results if r.kind == kind and r.resolved]
116	    return CalibrationMetrics(
117	        brier=round(brier_score(filtered), 6),
118	        log_loss=round(log_loss(filtered), 6),
119	        ece=round(ece(filtered), 6),
120	        reliability_bins=reliability_bins(filtered),
121	        resolution=round(resolution_score(filtered), 6),
122	        coverage=round(coverage(total_outcomes, len(filtered)), 6),
123	    )
124	
125	
126	def compute_metrics(
127	    results: list[ResolutionResult],
128	    total_outcomes_by_kind: dict[OutcomeKind, int],
129	) -> dict[OutcomeKind, CalibrationMetrics]:
130	    out: dict[OutcomeKind, CalibrationMetrics] = {}
131	    for kind, total in total_outcomes_by_kind.items():
132	        out[kind] = compute_metrics_for_kind(results, kind, total)
133	    return out
134

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import contextvars
4	import hashlib
5	import json
6	import logging
7	import os
8	import subprocess
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	from functools import wraps
11	from typing import Any, Callable
12	from uuid import uuid4
13	
14	from pydantic import BaseModel
15	
16	from noosphere.models import (
17	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
18	    Method,
19	    MethodImplRef,
20	    MethodInvocation,
21	    MethodType,
22	)
23	from noosphere.methods._hooks import _FAILURE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _PRE_HOOKS
24	from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	CORRELATION_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
29	    "CORRELATION_ID", default=""
30	)
31	TENANT_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
32	    "TENANT_ID", default="default"
33	)
34	
35	_store_factory: Callable[..., Any] | None = None
36	
37	
38	def set_store_factory(factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
39	    global _store_factory
40	    _store_factory = factory
41	
42	
43	def _get_store() -> Any:
44	    if _store_factory is not None:
45	        try:
46	            return _store_factory()
47	        except Exception:
48	            return None
49	    return None
50	
51	
52	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
53	    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
54	        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
55	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
56	
57	
58	def _current_git_sha() -> str:
59	    sha = os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA")
60	    if sha:
61	        return sha
62	    try:
63	        result = subprocess.run(
64	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
65	            capture_output=True,
66	            text=True,
67	            timeout=5,
68	        )
69	        if result.returncode == 0:
70	            return result.stdout.strip()
71	    except Exception:
72	        pass
73	    return "unknown"
74	
75	
76	def _current_correlation_id() -> str:
77	    return CORRELATION_ID.get()
78	
79	
80	def _current_tenant_id() -> str:
81	    return TENANT_ID.get()
82	
83	
84	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
85	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
86	        if isinstance(data, schema):
87	            return data
88	        return schema.model_validate(data)
89	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
90	        try:
91	            import jsonschema as _js
92	
93	            _js.validate(data, schema)
94	        except ImportError:
95	            pass
96	    return data
97	
98	
99	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
100	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
101	
102	
103	def _run_failure_path(
104	    spec: Method,
105	    inv: MethodInvocation,
106	    validated_input: Any,
107	    exc: BaseException,
108	) -> None:
109	    inv = inv.model_copy(
110	        update={
111	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
112	            "succeeded": False,
113	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
114	        }
115	    )
116	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
117	        try:
118	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
119	        except Exception:
120	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
121	    store = _get_store()
122	    if store is not None:
123	        try:
124	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
125	        except Exception:
126	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
127	
128	
129	def register_method(
130	    *,
131	    name: str,
132	    version: str,
133	    method_type: MethodType,
134	    input_schema: Any,
135	    output_schema: Any,
136	    description: str,
137	    rationale: str,
138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator
284
